action.reasoning: Observation: variance analysis reveals 99/128 target dimensions have variance <0.01, many near zero. This explains the huge negative R² for humidity tendencies—small prediction errors on near-zero-variance dims yield astronomically negative R². Training MSE is low (0.0035) but val MSE is huge (0.45), indicating the model lacks incentive to fit those low-variance dims well. Analysis: To maximize mean R², we must weight the loss inversely to per-dimension variance, forcing the model to accurately predict near-zero values for low-variance dims. The CNN architecture is reasonable but may benefit from increased capacity and residual connections. Decision: Implement a weighted MSE loss using precomputed per-dimension variance from the training set, cap weights to avoid numerical issues, and train the existing CNN with this loss. Also increase model size slightly and adjust early stopping to use mean R². Run compute-target-var then train for 15 epochs and submit eval to measure improvement.
